machinery_genes.role: hexameric inner tube; secreted hallmark structural subunit of T6SS-4
machinery_genes.quote: Furthermore, deletion of did not affect the secretion of Hcp4 (lower panel), indicating that this protein was not involved in substrate secretion by T6SS-4.
machinery_genes.note: Wang et al 2015 (PLoS Pathog), the characterisation paper for this YPIII T6SS-4 (the system whose effector YezP=YPK_3549 is curated for this instance). Hcp4 is the T6SS-4 Hcp (tube) protein; its secretion is the standard readout of a functional apparatus. Counted as machinery per the T6SS brief (Hcp is the secreted tube and belongs in the answer key).

machinery_genes.role: trimeric spike at the tube tip (cell-puncturing device) of T6SS-4
machinery_genes.quote: the conductance of the T6SS-4 channel is required for such resistance as the VgrG4-GFP fusion known to block T6SS secretion [] rendered the bacteria sensitive to HO
machinery_genes.note: Wang et al 2015 (PLoS Pathog). VgrG4 named as the VgrG of the YPIII T6SS-4; the VgrG4-GFP fusion is used as a dominant-negative that blocks T6SS-4 secretion, confirming it as the spike component of this system. Counted as machinery per the T6SS brief.

machinery_genes.role: AAA+ ATPase that powers/recycles the contractile sheath of T6SS-4
machinery_genes.quote: The acid tolerance phenotype of T6SS4 is dependent on the ATPase activity of ClpV4, one of the components of T6SS4.
machinery_genes.note: Zhang et al 2012 (Environ Microbiol), the founding paper that identified the YPIII T6SS4 (= T6SS-4). ClpV4 explicitly named as a component of T6SS4 with its ATPase activity required for function. TssH-family sheath-recycling ATPase; counted per the brief (count ClpV/TssH).

machinery_genes.quote: For TssF4 production, the sequence encoding the gene of fused to a 6xHis coding sequence was PCR amplified and cloned
machinery_genes.note: STRAIN TRANSFER: Gueguen et al 2013 (PLoS One) study the T6SS-4 cluster in Y. pseudotuberculosis strain IP31758, described there as 'a T6SS locus shared by' Y. pseudotuberculosis and Y. pestis, i.e. the same T6SS-4 family as the YPIII instance. They delete the essential apparatus gene in the IP31758 T6SS-4 cluster (boundaries YpsIP31758_3437 to _3420) and rescue the phenotype by producing a 6xHis-tagged TssF4 protein, naming TssF4 as a T6SS-4 baseplate component. Lower-confidence: named in IP31758, not the YPIII (YPK_) strain, though the locus is orthologous. The companion deletion gene in that cluster (YpsIP31758_3432, a tssK ortholog) is also an apparatus component but its name was not recoverable as a clean verbatim string from the retrieved full text, so it is not listed.

notes: System: T6SS-4 is one of four complete T6SS gene clusters in Y. pseudotuberculosis. The YPIII genome (NC_010465.1) is the strain used by the Shen lab (Northwest A&F University), whose curated effectors for this instance are YezP (YPK_3549) and YPK_3548. T6SS-4 is a non-canonical, thermoregulated (induced at 26-28C), stress-response T6SS that transports metal-ion-binding substrates (Zn2+-binding YezP) rather than acting mainly in interbacterial killing. Founding/characterisation papers: Zhang et al 2012 (identified T6SS4, named ClpV4) and Wang et al 2015 (Zn2+ transport, named Hcp4 and VgrG4 as the T6SS-4 tube and spike). Machinery genes confidently named in the literature for THIS instance: the secreted tube Hcp4 (TssD), the spike VgrG4 (TssI), and the ATPase ClpV4 (TssH). TssF4 (baseplate) is added via the separated-paper/strain-transfer rule from Gueguen et al 2013, who characterise the orthologous T6SS-4 cluster in strain IP31758. Status = PARTIAL: only ~4 of the ~13 core Tss components are named with quotes; the remaining membrane-complex (TssJLM/IcmF), baseplate (TssE/G/K), and sheath (TssB/C, VipA/B) genes are present in the cluster but were not individually named with a defensible verbatim quote in the literature I could access. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED as non-machinery: the delivered effector substrates YezP/YPK_3549 (Zn2+ chelator), YPK_3548, TssS/micropeptide Mn2+ chelator (Zhu et al 2021), and the Tce1 nuclease (Song et al 2021, which belongs to T6SS-3 not T6SS-4); all regulators (OmpR, OxyR, ZntR, RovA/RovM, RelA, HpaR, RpoS, quorum sensing). The generic statement in Wang 2015 that 'ClpV and IcmF ... are crucial for the secretion of Hcp, VgrG' names IcmF only as a conserved T6SS component in general, not specifically as an IP-/YPK-tagged T6SS-4 gene, so IcmF4 is not listed. All quotes are verbatim from the cited PMC full texts / PubMed abstracts; minor blanks in quotes correspond to italicised gene-name tags that were stripped during full-text retrieval.
